commit.message:
Fix first-prompt status stuck on "typing" instead of "processing" (#330)

Pool sessions always have idle signals when idle. When the signal is
missing for a non-activated pool session, UserPromptSubmit must have
cleared it — treat as processing. Previously fell through to a
transcriptContains check that failed due to JSONL race + fingerprint
caching, keeping the session stuck on "typing" until the Stop hook
wrote back the idle signal.

* Rewrite fullscreen: use wrapper element instead of fullscreening chart directly

Move the chart into a temporary wrapper div for fullscreen, then move it
back to its original position on exit. Use Plotly.relayout with explicit
width/height for fullscreen, then null to restore autosize on exit.
This avoids Plotly's internal inline styles persisting after exit.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NdcxvwbkBpWGsYcNQeGtW3

* Fix fullscreen exit: clear inline dimensions and dispatch resize event

After moving chart back to its flex container, clear all inline
width/height on Plotly's internal elements before calling relayout.
Then dispatch a window resize event so ALL responsive charts in the
flex layout recalculate their dimensions properly.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NdcxvwbkBpWGsYcNQeGtW3

* Fix flex layout distortion: zero-width element before relayout

After moving chart back into flex container, temporarily set width:0
and min-width:0 so the element doesn't influence flex space distribution.
Then on next animation frame, clear those styles and let Plotly relayout
to the correct container width. This prevents the residual fullscreen
pixel width from distorting sibling flex items.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NdcxvwbkBpWGsYcNQeGtW3

* Fix fullscreen exit: purge and recreate chart from saved data

Instead of trying to reset Plotly's internal state, save the original
data/layout before fullscreen, then on exit use Plotly.purge + newPlot
to completely rebuild the chart from scratch. This guarantees no
residual fullscreen dimensions affect the flex layout.

* Fix fullscreen: placeholder preserves flex space + resize siblings

Root cause: when chart is removed from flex container for fullscreen,
the sibling chart expands to fill all space. When chart returns, the
sibling stays expanded.

Fix:
1. Placeholder gets flex:1 and min-width/height matching the removed
   element, preventing sibling from expanding
2. After recreating the chart, resize all sibling Plotly charts in
   the same parent to recalculate their correct dimensions

fix: prevent infinite helmet rescue loop on Charred_Melee

Vanilla SetHelmetItem skips re-attach when m_helmetItem already equals
the target name. The rescue coroutine was triggering a loop because each
re-equip attempt left the field set to "HelmetDrake", causing subsequent
calls to be no-ops. Fix: clear m_helmetItem before the rescue call so
vanilla treats it as a new item. Also cap rescue attempts at 3 to guard
against any future failure modes.

commit.message:
Evolve indras-relay into authenticated three-tier relay node

Add credential-based authentication and three-tier storage staging
(Self/Connections/Public) to the relay server. Each tier has independent
quotas, TTLs, and access controls matching the Indras social model.

New modules:
- auth.rs: Ed25519 credential validation, session tracking, tier assignment
- tier.rs: Tier determination logic mapping players to access levels

Wire protocol additions (indras-transport):
- StorageTier enum, RelayAuth/RelayAuthAck/RelayStore/RelayStoreAck messages
- StoreMetadata, StoreContentType, TierQuotaInfo types

Key changes:
- BlobStore uses separate redb tables per tier (6 tables total)
- TieredQuotaManager enforces per-tier byte and interface limits
- RelayNode requires authentication before Register/Retrieve/Store
- Cleanup task uses per-tier TTLs (Self: 365d, Connections: 90d, Public: 7d)
- Admin API reports per-tier storage stats and peer tier access
- Config supports owner_player_id for personal vs community server mode

40 relay tests + 62 transport tests pass, 0 failures.

Route attention reads/writes to DM realm for community intentions

Community intentions live in DM realms shared between peers. Previously,
attention was always written to and read from the home realm, so peers
could never see each other's attention on shared intentions.

write to the DM realm when set — so both peers see each other's
attention bars.

them on exit. Also clear any inline dimensions Plotly may have set on the
gd element before calling resize.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NdcxvwbkBpWGsYcNQeGtW3

* Rewrite fullscreen: use wrapper element instead of fullscreening chart directly

Move the chart into a temporary wrapper div for fullscreen, then move it
back to its original position on exit. Use Plotly.relayout with explicit
width/height for fullscreen, then null to restore autosize on exit.
This avoids Plotly's internal inline styles persisting after exit.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NdcxvwbkBpWGsYcNQeGtW3

* Fix fullscreen exit: clear inline dimensions and dispatch resize event

After moving chart back to its flex container, clear all inline
width/height on Plotly's internal elements before calling relayout.
Then dispatch a window resize event so ALL responsive charts in the
flex layout recalculate their dimensions properly.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NdcxvwbkBpWGsYcNQeGtW3

* Fix flex layout distortion: zero-width element before relayout

After moving chart back into flex container, temporarily set width:0
and min-width:0 so the element doesn't influence flex space distribution.
Then on next animation frame, clear those styles and let Plotly relayout
to the correct container width. This prevents the residual fullscreen
pixel width from distorting sibling flex items.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NdcxvwbkBpWGsYcNQeGtW3

* Fix fullscreen exit: purge and recreate chart from saved data

Instead of trying to reset Plotly's internal state, save the original
data/layout before fullscreen, then on exit use Plotly.purge + newPlot
to completely rebuild the chart from scratch. This guarantees no
residual fullscreen dimensions affect the flex layout.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NdcxvwbkBpWGsYcNQeGtW3

* Fix fullscreen: placeholder preserves flex space + resize siblings

Root cause: when chart is removed from flex container for fullscreen,
the sibling chart expands to fill all space. When chart returns, the
sibling stays expanded.

Fix:
1. Placeholder gets flex:1 and min-width/height matching the removed
   element, preventing sibling from expanding
2. After recreating the chart, resize all sibling Plotly charts in
   the same parent to recalculate their correct dimensions
